name: Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

rent.notes: Philadelphia has no residential rental tax (commercial rentals taxed at 8. Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills (not included in rent).
rent.gigInternetNotes: Fios widely available in Philadelphia proper and most rowhouse neighborhoods. Some gaps in West/Southwest Philly. ~85% fiber coverage.

electricity.notes: PECO Energy. Moderate A/C; some electric heat in older homes.

gas.notes: PGW (Philadelphia Gas Works). Primary heating, cooking.

water.notes: Philadelphia Water Dept. Among highest water rates in US. Tiered pricing.

sewage.notes: Billed with water. Philadelphia stormwater + sewer charges.

trash.notes: City of Philadelphia Streets Dept. Included in city services for most residences.

utilities.taxes: Pennsylvania exempts residential utilities from 6% sales tax.
utilities.notes: Electricity (heating+cooling), gas, water, sewage, trash. Internet is in entertainment (Cable+1Gig).

healthcare.notes: Medicare Part B ($174.70/mo) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo x2 people) + Part D (~$45/mo x2) + dental/vision plans. Higher premiums reflect macular degeneration specialist coverage and frequent monitoring needs.

petCare.notes: Vet visits, vaccinations, flea/tick, pet insurance; Penn Vet nearby

petDaycare.notes: Doggy daycare 2 days/week, large breed rate (~$44/day). Camp Bow Wow, Zoom Room, Lucky Dog Daycare. Prices vary by neighborhood.

petGrooming.notes: Bernese coat maintenance, professional grooming monthly

transportation.notes: SEPTA transit + walkable center city; car optional

entertainment.notes: Incl cable+1Gig internet, Netflix, Amazon Prime, Apple TV+, dining out (lunch for 2 2x/week w/ tax+tip)

phoneCell.notes: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials. 2 phones, 1.5GB+ high-speed data each, all taxes included.

buffer.notes: Monthly safety buffer for unexpected expenses

medicalOOP.notes: Anti-VEGF injections for macular degeneration (~$300-400 OOP each, 6-10x/yr after Medicare 80%). Specialist visits: ophthalmologist q6-8wks, endocrinologist 2-4x/yr, cardiologist 1-2x/yr. Frequent labs: lipid panel, TSH, A1C, CMP 3-4x/yr each. Both adults: PCP visits quarterly.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

healthcarePreMedicare.notes: ACA silver benchmark for 2-adult household both age ~60, before subsidies. County-level estimate (Philadelphia County, PA). Pennie — Philadelphia metro most competitive in PA. Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

stateIncomeTax.exemptions: SS and all retirement income exempt from PA tax. City wage tax 3.75% on earned income only (not retirement).

taxes.notes: PA exempts all retirement income from state tax.

acaMarketplace.notes: Pennie — Philadelphia metro most competitive in PA.
acaMarketplace.disclaimer: Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

pros: PA exempts SS and retirement income from state tax
